_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-44R Tranexamic Acid (TXA) (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-44R: Tranexamic Acid — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
indications: Traumatic hemorrhage — significant bleeding in patients with injury within 3 hours
contraindications: Injury >3 hours before administration (evidence suggests harm after 3 hours — do NOT give)
Suspected intracranial bleeding (relative — avoid; contact online MC)
Active thromboembolic disease (DVT, PE, arterial thrombosis — relative)
precautions: Adults >18 years only — Michigan EMS protocol does not include pediatric dosing
Time-critical: must be within 3 hours of injury for benefit; do NOT delay transport to administer
TXA prehospital is the FIRST DOSE — hospital will administer second dose; communicate TXA given to receiving facility
Label IV bag clearly with fluorescent 'TXA added' label so receiving facility knows first dose has been given
Administer as infusion over 10 minutes — do NOT rapid bolus (convulsions and hypotension risk with rapid infusion)
expected_effects: Reduction in fibrinolysis — prevents breakdown of existing clot
Reduced mortality in significant traumatic hemorrhage when given early
No significant hemostatic effect beyond 3 hours from injury
side_effects: Seizures (with rapid infusion or very high doses)
notes: Dose: 1 gram in 100 mL NS piggyback infusion over 10 minutes
Injury must be within 3 hours — do NOT give if >3 hours
Label IV bag with fluorescent 'TXA added' label
TXA is FIRST DOSE prehospital — hospital must be notified and will give second dose
Do NOT delay transport to administer — infuse during transport

dosing.population: Adult >18 yr — traumatic hemorrhage within 3 hours of injury
dosing.dose: 1 gram in 100 mL NS; infuse over 10 minutes (piggyback)
dosing.notes: Within 3 hours of injury ONLY; label IV bag 'TXA added'; notify receiving hospital; do NOT delay transport; adults >18 yr only
